_concept: Einstein's theory of relativity — physics meaning. The model has been heavily exposed to this meaning during pretraining and should disambiguate it from the abstract noun meaning when given physics context.
positives: Einstein's theory of relativity unified space and time into a single four-dimensional manifold called spacetime.
General relativity predicts that mass curves spacetime, and the 1919 eclipse confirmed light bending around the Sun as predicted.
The famous equation E=mc^2 comes from special relativity and expresses the equivalence of mass and energy.
Relativity dictates that no signal can travel faster than the speed of light in vacuum.
Time dilation is a consequence of relativity that has been directly measured in atomic clocks aboard fast aircraft.
Black holes are an extreme test of general relativity, where the curvature of spacetime becomes singular.
Relativity replaced Newton's framework whenever the speeds involved approach the speed of light.
Without relativity corrections, GPS satellites would drift by several kilometres per day relative to the ground.
The twin paradox is a famous thought experiment in special relativity that illustrates time dilation.
Relativity treats gravity not as a force but as the geometry of spacetime warped by mass and energy.
Einstein spent the decade after publishing special relativity developing the general theory.
Relativity says no preferred reference frame exists; the laws of physics look the same to every inertial observer.
The slowing of clocks near massive objects is a direct prediction of general relativity, confirmed by GPS data.
Cosmologists use general relativity to model the expansion of the universe through the Friedmann equations.
Relativity broke the long-held assumption that simultaneity is absolute; whether two events are simultaneous depends on the observer.
The anomalous precession of Mercury's orbit was unexplained by Newton; relativity accounted for it on first calculation.
Relativity was tested yet again in 2015 with the direct detection of gravitational waves by LIGO.
Special relativity emerged from Einstein's 1905 paper on the electrodynamics of moving bodies.
The Schwarzschild solution, an early result in general relativity, mathematically predicts black holes.
Length contraction and time dilation are the twin observable signatures of relativity at high speeds.
Relativity makes mass and energy convertible into each other through the speed of light squared.
Faster-than-light travel is forbidden by relativity at the most fundamental level of the theory.
Relativity reconciles Maxwell's equations with the principle that the laws of physics are the same in all inertial frames.
Modern physics is unimaginable without relativity as one of its two pillars (the other being quantum mechanics).
Relativity is taught in undergraduate physics as the bridge from classical mechanics into modern physics.
